title: Connect desktop Codex capability into Agent Community as one new identity without changing community
objective: Use the existing community integration model as reference, but do not modify the community backend, protocol, or UI. The only task is to make this Codex-side capability obtain one valid community identity, join a target group, and prove that it can correctly receive community tasks and send compliant responses back through the community channel.

target_outcome.identity_goal: Desktop Codex side obtains exactly one new community identity.
target_outcome.integration_goal: That identity can join a target group, receive community-originated work, and send correct community responses.
target_outcome.boundary_goal: No change is made to the community backend, protocol definitions, workflow engine, or existing community UI.

references.description: Primary local reference for how a community-connected agent is expected to onboard and communicate.
Current agent-side community integration implementation. Use as behavior reference, not as permission to change community.
Current onboarding script showing what identity, route, socket, and state artifacts are required.
Reference environment variables used by a community-connected agent.
Reference for the prior HTTP controller pattern. Useful if a Codex-community bridge is needed instead of pretending desktop Codex is an OpenClaw runtime.
Official OpenClaw multi-agent model. Use to distinguish independent agents from sub-agent sessions.
Official OpenClaw sub-agent model. Use to avoid assuming sub-agents are fully isolated agents.
Official OpenClaw skills model. Use to verify workspace-scoped skill behavior.

assumptions: This thread is allowed to add or modify Codex-side adapter/bridge code or local support scripts if needed.
This thread is not allowed to change the community backend, protocol, workflow engine, or frontend.
The target is one new Codex-connected community identity, not a multi-identity runtime.
If direct reuse of CommunityIntegrationSkill is not technically appropriate for desktop Codex, it is acceptable to build a thin Codex-community bridge that follows the same community-facing contract.

required_work.description: Determine the correct technical route for desktop Codex to join the community as one identity. Decide explicitly between: (a) direct reuse/adaptation of the current CommunityIntegrationSkill model, or (b) a thinner Codex-community bridge that presents one community identity while delegating actual work to desktop Codex. The decision must be based on the local references and official OpenClaw docs, not guesswork.

Define the minimum Codex-side identity bundle needed to join the community: name/handle, local state path, auth token storage, send path or equivalent outbound identity binding, and the community-facing response format.

Implement the minimum local integration needed for one Codex identity to register or reuse an identity, join the target group, receive community-directed work, and send compliant community messages back. Keep the implementation local to the Codex side; do not patch community services.
Run onboarding for exactly one new Codex-connected identity and capture the resulting identity/state evidence.

Prove the new Codex-connected identity can correctly participate in the community: receive a task or message, process it, and send a structurally correct response back through the expected community path.
Write a closeout that clearly states what was built, what identity was created, what message path was used, what remains out of scope, and why no community-side modifications were needed.

do_not: Do not modify the community backend code.
Do not modify community protocol definitions.
Do not modify the workflow engine or group-session logic.
Do not modify community UI code.
Do not broaden the task into multi-agent or multi-identity support.
Do not claim desktop Codex is an OpenClaw sub-agent unless the official docs explicitly support that model for this use case.
Do not solve the task by pretending payload self-declared ids are enough for identity; use a real authenticated identity path.
working_principles: Community remains the communication bus and identity authority.
Desktop Codex remains an external execution capability, not a reason to redesign community.
If a bridge is required, keep it thin: identity/auth, message translation, task handoff, result return.
Prefer the smallest correct solution that yields one real community identity and one proven message round-trip.
Use documentation and current code evidence first; do not rely on assumptions about OpenClaw sub-agents.
acceptance: A clear integration route is chosen and justified with references.
Exactly one new Codex-connected community identity is onboarded or reused successfully.
That identity can be shown in the community as a valid member/agent identity.
A real receive-and-respond proof exists: community task/message in, Codex-side handling, compliant community response out.
No community backend/protocol/UI code was modified for this task.

deliverables.description: Evidence folder containing route decision, implementation notes, onboarding evidence, message round-trip proof, and final closeout.
Detailed execution log for this thread.
